document_name: Project Charter for Nationwide Crate Recovery Initiative
description: Formal document authorizing the project, defining high-level scope, outlining success criteria (40% volume, 108,000 crates), budget ceiling (4M DKK), and identifying primary stakeholders and high-level risks based on the 'Builder' strategy.
steps_to_create: Incorporate SMART criteria from the Goal Statement.
Define the 'Builder' strategy's core choices (60/40 split, subsidies, centralized hubs).
Obtain formal sign-off from Arla Foods Executive Sponsor.

document_name: Current State Assessment of Danish Crate Loss & Recovery Feasibility
description: Initial strategic report analyzing existing crate loss patterns, estimating the exact volume distribution between supermarket returns and municipal stations, and proposing preliminary workload estimates for the three QA Hubs, crucial for budget allocation (Risk 3 modeling).
steps_to_create: Synthesize historical loss data and integrate preliminary volume hypotheses for the 60/40 split.
Model initial throughput and required cleaning cycles for 108,000 units.
Develop initial cost-per-return estimate for both collection channels based on logistical friction analysis.

document_name: Framework for Consumer Incentive Structure Calibration
description: Policy document outlining the rationale, structure, and budgetary limits for the DKK 5 fixed charitable donation incentive, addressing its behavioral impact and defining trigger points for emergency calibration (e.g., increasing incentive if Q3 returns lag).
steps_to_create: Document the DKK 5 fixed incentive and the 1.35M DKK maximum donation outlay.
Define the quantitative trigger (e.g., 2 weeks below projected volume) for mandatory review.
Outline budgetary sweep mechanism from Marketing contingency to increase incentive value.

description: A concentrated risk register focusing on risks critical to the Q2 pilot and Q3 launch dependencies (Regulatory Timeline, Municipal SLAs, Identification Failure). It formalizes the mitigation actions prioritized in 'expert-review.md' and 'swot-analysis.md'.
steps_to_create: Finalize risk tolerances based on expert consultation.
Formally document mandatory actions for Regulatory (EPA) and Municipal SLA timelines.
Allocate specific budget contingences (e.g., 150k DKK for EPA testing) within the register.

document_name: Stakeholder Engagement Strategy for Municipalities & Retailers
description: Detailed plan outlining engagement methods, required negotiation points (SLAs, subsidies), and communication tone tailored to Supermarket Chains and Municipal Waste Authorities, based on minimizing friction (Builder strategy). Includes structure for the Municipal Liaison Manager's activities.
steps_to_create: Define the capacity coefficient requirement for Municipal SLAs (Risk 1 mitigation).
Finalize the structure of the supermarket performance subsidy versus the mandatory compliance fee (Risk 2 mitigation).
Schedule initial negotiation meetings with key retail and municipal counterparts.

document_name: Draft Service Level Agreement (SLA) Template for Municipal Recycling Stations
description: The primary contractual document template required for securing 60% of return volume. Must incorporate capacity coefficients, defined drop-off procedures, and preliminary penalty clauses to safeguard against operational failure (Risk 1).
steps_to_create: Incorporate specific capacity coefficient requirements based on expert modeling.
Include review clauses addressing Danish waste classification (EPR/Taxes).
Secure initial review by Legal Counsel.

document_name: Narrative Strategy & ID Contingency Plan Framework
description: High-level framework defining the dual communication approach: the primary provocative narrative ('Garden Furniture') and the immediate digital/physical contingency plan for consumer identification failure (Risk 4).
steps_to_create: Finalize the 'Heroic Recovery' counter-framing to balance provocative initial messaging.
Define the quantitative trigger (10% contamination) for Physical ID Contingency deployment.
Liaise with Incentive Specialist to commission required digital/physical assets.
approval_authorities: Project Sponsor, Public Engagement & PR Narrative Architect

description: A document establishing the primary financial breakdown based on assumptions (34% Donation, 45% Logistics, 21% Marketing) and ring-fencing the initial contingency buffer (DKK 200k) for operational risk management.
steps_to_create: Formalize the allocation split against the 4M DKK total.
Document the DKK 1.35M maximum donation ceiling as a hard stop.
Establish the initial DKK 200k Risk Management Contingency Fund.

description: Document required by the Reverse Logistics & QA Operations Lead to begin securing provisional leases and sourcing equipment for the three centralized QA hubs, contingent upon pilot data and regulatory clearance.
steps_to_create: Identify three provisional site locations based on population density.
Define layout/workflow requirements for cleaning/sorting based on expected contamination.
Initiate procurement process for essential site lease booking reserves by May 30th.

description: Defines the key performance indicators (KPIs) and reporting cadence required to track the four core objectives (Volume, CO2, Impressions, Budget) prior to the development of a detailed measurement plan.
responsible_role_type: Pilot Program Coordinator & Field Test Lead
steps_to_create: Map the four primary success metrics (Volume, CO2, Impressions, Budget) to appropriate data sources.
Establish the monthly reporting schedule starting Q2 pilot feedback.
Define the threshold for success/failure for each KPI.

document_name: Logistical Cost-Per-Return Model: Municipal vs. Supermarket Channel Analysis
description: Detailed spreadsheet model comparing the projected cost profiles (transport, handling, personnel) for the 60% municipal volume vs. the 40% supermarket volume, explicitly addressing expert concerns about municipal gate fees and transport optimization for the QA hubs.
steps_to_create: Integrate findings from the 'Current State Assessment' with initial SLA cost estimates.
Model potential budget impact of undisclosed municipal gate fees (Risk 1 mitigation).
Establish target cost ceiling for both channels to trigger pivot recommendations.

document_name: Digital Verification Tool Design & Deployment Plan (Placeholder)
description: Initial requirements and wireframe sketches for the 'Killer Application' (QR-coded bonus) identified in the SWOT analysis. This plan will define the user flow, required backend integration for donation bonus tracking, and the Q2 pilot deployment timeline.
steps_to_create: Define non-downloadable delivery method (SMS/Web-App).
Specify data points required for the QR scan (location correlation).
Liaise with Incentive Specialist to confirm bonus structure.

document_name: Policy Brief: Regulatory Hard-Stop Protocol for Cleaning Certification Delay
description: Definitive action plan detailing immediate operational shifts if Danish EPA certification (Dependency 1) is not secured by the target date (July 15th). It specifies the inventory hold point and criteria for rerouting uncertified crates to non-food recycling, neutralizing the CO2 value but maintaining logistics flow.
steps_to_create: Define the specific contamination/volume threshold that triggers the hard-stop protocol.
Outline the logistical chain required to divert uncertified material to recycling partners.
Calculate the financial impact of foregoing CO2 savings for uncertified crates.
approval_authorities: Project Sponsor, Reverse Logistics & QA Operations Lead
